note: Michigan has a state cover page in front matter (pp. 5-6) but no separate addendum in Exhibit G. Indiana, South Dakota, Wisconsin, and Hawaii are listed in Exhibit A (state administrators) but do NOT have addenda in Exhibit G.

overrides.override_summary: Post-term covenant not to compete may not be enforceable under California law
overrides.why_it_matters: California generally bars noncompetes; the 2-year/20-mile post-term restriction may be unenforceable for CA franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Utah arbitration venue may not be enforceable under California law (Bus. & Prof. Code 20040.5, CCP 1281)
overrides.why_it_matters: CA franchisees may be able to arbitrate/litigate in California rather than Utah

overrides.override_summary: Utah choice of law may not be enforceable under California law
overrides.why_it_matters: CA franchise relationship law may govern instead of Utah law

overrides.override_summary: Termination upon bankruptcy may not be enforceable under federal bankruptcy law; CA Bus. & Prof. Code 20000-20043 provides termination/transfer/nonrenewal rights
overrides.why_it_matters: CA franchisees have statutory protections beyond those in the franchise agreement

overrides.override_summary: Material modification to franchise agreement requires approved disclosure document from Commissioner
overrides.why_it_matters: Franchisor cannot unilaterally modify key terms without regulatory oversight

overrides.override_summary: Any action must be brought in state or federal court of general jurisdiction in Illinois
overrides.why_it_matters: IL franchisees need not travel to Utah for litigation

overrides.override_summary: Illinois law shall apply (FA addendum); notwithstanding, ADA arbitration stays at agreement location
overrides.why_it_matters: Illinois franchise law protections apply rather than Utah law

overrides.override_summary: Any provision requiring waiver of rights under Illinois Franchise Disclosure Act or other IL law is void
overrides.why_it_matters: Cannot waive statutory franchise protections

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee's rights upon termination and non-renewal governed by Sections 19 and 20 of the IL Franchise Disclosure Act
overrides.why_it_matters: Statutory protections override agreement provisions on termination/non-renewal

overrides.override_summary: General release required for renewal/sale/transfer shall not apply to liability under MD Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law
overrides.why_it_matters: MD franchisees retain ability to bring MD franchise law claims even after signing releases

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee may bring lawsuit in Maryland for claims arising under MD law
overrides.why_it_matters: Can litigate MD franchise law claims in MD courts

overrides.override_summary: Termination upon bankruptcy may not be enforceable under federal bankruptcy law
overrides.why_it_matters: Bankruptcy protection preserved

overrides.override_summary: Cannot require litigation outside Minnesota or waiver of jury trial
overrides.why_it_matters: MN franchisees can litigate in MN and retain jury trial rights

overrides.override_summary: Cannot require consent to liquidated damages, termination penalties, or judgment notes
overrides.why_it_matters: The 24-month royalty liquidated damages clause may be unenforceable for MN franchisees

overrides.override_summary: 90 days' notice of termination (60 to cure); 180 days' notice of non-renewal
overrides.why_it_matters: Significantly longer notice and cure periods than the 3-30 day cure periods in the base agreement

overrides.override_summary: Franchisor cannot require franchisee to assent to a general release
overrides.why_it_matters: No release required on renewal or transfer — preserves all claims

overrides.override_summary: No waiver of claims under state franchise law or disclaimer of reliance on franchisor statements
overrides.why_it_matters: Fraud-in-the-inducement claims preserved; reliance on representations cannot be disclaimed

overrides.override_summary: NSF fees capped at $30 per Minnesota Statute 604.113
overrides.why_it_matters: Limits financial penalties for bounced payments

overrides.override_summary: NY General Business Law Article 33 applies; additional disclosure requirements
overrides.why_it_matters: NY franchisees benefit from state-specific franchise disclosure protections

overrides.override_summary: Liquidated damages clauses and termination penalties may be unenforceable under ND law
overrides.why_it_matters: 24-month royalty liquidated damages clause may not apply to ND franchisees

overrides.override_summary: All noncompete covenants subject to NDCC Section 9-08-06
overrides.why_it_matters: ND has strict limits on noncompetes; the 2-year/20-mile restriction may be limited

overrides.override_summary: Utah mediation/arbitration/litigation venue may not be enforceable under ND law
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchisees may be able to resolve disputes in ND

overrides.override_summary: Utah choice of law may not be enforceable under ND law
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchise law may govern instead of Utah law

overrides.override_summary: Provision restricting jurisdiction or venue to a forum outside RI is void with respect to claims under RI Franchise Investment Act
overrides.why_it_matters: RI franchisees can bring RI franchise law claims in RI courts

overrides.override_summary: Requiring application of laws of another state is void with respect to RI Franchise Investment Act claims
overrides.why_it_matters: RI franchise law applies to RI franchise law claims regardless of Utah choice-of-law provision

overrides.override_summary: Termination upon bankruptcy may not be enforceable under federal bankruptcy law
overrides.why_it_matters: Bankruptcy protection preserved for VA franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Washington Franchise Investment Protection Act (RCW 19.100) prevails in case of conflict
overrides.why_it_matters: WA franchise law overrides Utah law for WA franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration/mediation in WA or mutually agreed location; litigation can be brought in WA for franchise sale/WFIPA claims
overrides.why_it_matters: WA franchisees need not travel to Utah for dispute resolution

overrides.override_summary: Release/waiver void unless pursuant to negotiated settlement with independent counsel after agreement in effect (RCW 19.100.220(2))
overrides.why_it_matters: Releases on renewal/transfer are void unless negotiated with lawyers after the fact

overrides.override_summary: Noncompete void for employees earning <$100K/year and contractors earning <$250K/year (RCW 49.62); nonsolicitation restrictions between franchisees and franchisor void (RCW 49.62.060)
overrides.why_it_matters: Most employee/contractor-level noncompetes unenforceable in WA; cannot restrict inter-franchisee hiring

overrides.override_summary: Treble damages available under RCW 19.100.190; waivers of exemplary/punitive damages void except in negotiated post-execution settlements
overrides.why_it_matters: WA franchisees can seek treble damages; franchisor's waiver provisions are void

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee may terminate under any grounds permitted by state law; buy-back without consent unlawful unless terminated for good cause (RCW 19.100.180(2)(j))
overrides.why_it_matters: WA franchisees have additional termination rights and protections against forced buy-backs

overrides.override_summary: No questionnaire or acknowledgment can waive state franchise law claims or disclaim reliance on franchisor statements
overrides.why_it_matters: Fraud-in-the-inducement claims and reliance on representations preserved
